cases.alignment_rate_expected_with_live_timeli: 0.0 to 0.5 depending on how many frames have target_lane='right' vs 'current' in baseline
cases.warning_level: false_positive — 1.0 is artifact of synthetic preferred_lane='current'
cases.run_to_run_stability: deterministic at 1.0 every run with synthetic frames
cases.remediation: Wire live stage3b_behavior_timeline. Fix _synthesize_frames_from_frozen() to emit target_lane = corridor.preferred_lane.
cases.note: Current 1.0 is NOT a good signal. It means the metric is not measuring anything useful for this case.

cases.alignment_rate_expected_with_live_timeli: 1.0 — route_option=straight → preferred_lane always 'current' → all frames eligible and aligned by definition
cases.warning_level: acceptable — metric is trivially correct but uninformative
cases.remediation: None needed for contract gate. For signal quality: acceptable as is. Alignment metric is not useful for straight-route cases.
cases.note: 1.0 is technically correct. The metric alignment signal is zero-information for straight-route stop/follow cases. Do not attempt to improve this by changing threshold.

cases.warning_level: IMPORTANT — will breach soft_guardrail threshold 0.65. But this is structurally expected: when baseline arbitrates from LC to stop, target_lane legitimately reverts to 'current'.
cases.run_to_run_stability: deterministic — split-phase pattern repeats every run identically
cases.remediation: Two options: (1) Accept as soft warning — the metric is correctly detecting the arbitration-phase split, not a real error. (2) Apply per-case threshold override: alignment threshold for arbitration_stop = 0.30 (not 0.65).
cases.is_real_warning: SOFT — breach is expected and structurally explained. NOT a contract issue.
cases.note: This case will produce the most analytically interesting alignment data in the expansion. The pattern (high alignment in prepare-phase, low in stop-phase) is the signal to track.

cases.status: provisional — NOT in current expansion set
cases.breach_severity: SEVERE — systematic 0.0-0.10 on every run, every frame
cases.warning_level: HIGH — will always breach threshold. But this is structurally inherent: stop-dominated case with preferred_lane != 'current' always misaligns.
cases.remediation: Must apply per-case threshold exemption OR exclude alignment metric from gate for stop-dominated cases. Do NOT change global threshold.
cases.is_real_warning: NO — this is a metric design issue: alignment metric is not appropriate for stop-dominated cases where preferred_lane='right' but target_lane is correctly 'current' during stop behavior.
cases.important_status_note: status=provisional. Do NOT add to expansion set before status=ready.

cases_with_false_positive_pass: ml_right_positive_core (1.0 via synthetic frames — not real)
cross_case_summary.intent_causing_most_misalignment: stop_before_obstacle, follow (both emit target_lane='current' while preferred_lane='right')
